payload.input: Your parents will be so proud."  Behind them, in the closet above, a soft, rhythmic sound begins—barely audible, the wet friction of skin on skin, Julian’s hand between Vivian’s legs as they watch through the slats, her teeth sunk into his shoulder to muffle her breathing. The sound is drowned by the air conditioning, by the rustle of Pastor Jim’s polyester pants as he shifts, but Maya hears it in her bones, feels it in the heat pooling between her thighs. "We should pray," Ethan says, his voice higher than usual, his eyes darting toward the ceiling. He kneels on the carpet, his khakis pulling tight across his thighs, the outline of his erection still visible if one knew to look. "Before you go. A prayer of... of thanksgiving."
